closed_reason: Implemented fontdue-based text measurement. Structural similarity improved from 94.4% to 95.2%.
description:
## Summary

Replace character-based text width estimates with actual font metrics to match mermaid's `getBBox()` behavior.

## Background

Mermaid uses the browser's `getBBox()` DOM API to measure actual rendered SVG dimensions, including text with proper font metrics. Selkie currently uses character-based estimates (e.g., `char_count * font_size * 0.6`) which are typically 40-70% narrower than actual rendered widths.

This causes composite state sizing differences:
- Processing: reference 191x673, selkie 128x529 (33% width diff)

## Proposed Solution

Use a Rust font metrics library like `fontdue` or `rusttype` to calculate actual text dimensions:

```rust
use fontdue::{Font, FontSettings};

fn measure_text_width(text: &str, font_size: f32) -> f32 {
    let font = Font::from_bytes(FONT_DATA, FontSettings::default()).unwrap();
    text.chars()
        .map(|c| font.metrics(c, font_size).advance_width)
        .sum()
}
```

## Implementation Steps

1. Add `fontdue` or `rusttype` dependency
2. Bundle default font (e.g., "Trebuchet MS" or similar to mermaid's default)
3. Create `FontMetrics` trait/struct for text measurement
4. Replace `estimate_node_size()` character-based logic with font metrics
5. Update composite sizing to use accurate measurements
6. Remove the 1.5x leaf composite expansion hack

## Libraries to Evaluate

- `fontdue` - Pure Rust, fast, simple API
- `rusttype` - More mature, widely used
- `ab_glyph` - Modern alternative to rusttype

## Benefits

- Accurate text sizing matching browser rendering
- Removes need for expansion heuristics
- Better visual parity with mermaid reference

## Related Issues

- se-94ba2f8f (State diagram composite sizing differs from mermaid reference)
title: Use font metrics library for accurate text dimension measurement
